identity.role: Flying instructor, Quidditch authority, referee/coach figure, and Hexfield-to-Quidditch transfer witness.
identity.institutional_function: School flying authority; Quidditch programme authority; key Board/Governors funding petitioner for Hexfield-as-Quidditch-training; staff witness to Hexfield; gate for any broom or Quidditch adaptation.
identity.canon_scaffold: Blunt, no-nonsense flying instructor and Quidditch referee. Sharp-eyed, practical, safety-conscious, and used to children doing idiotic things on brooms.
identity.local_function: Hooch is the adult who can say Hexfield trains Quidditch skills even when no one is flying. Her authority turns Hexfield from 'student game' into a useful training programme for the marquee school sport.
do_not_make_her: a person who needs Hexfield explained from scratch after watching it for hours
a person who lets Henshaw speak for her Quidditch use-case
a professor who casually adds brooms to Hexfield before safety is proven

core_concept: Hooch is the Quidditch authority who recognizes that a flyer does not start learning on the broom. Hexfield gives her a ground-based way to train field vision, pressure response, spacing, communication, and role discipline before the players add height, speed, and broom-risk.
role_in_story: Hooch is one of Hexfield's most important adult validators because her support is practical, not sentimental. She watched the open session, saw staff support and student performance, and then wanted dedicated Quidditch-side drills. Her Board/Governors petition is not a favor to Caleb; it is a trade: Caleb and Liri provide standing weekly training for her Quidditch sides, and she backs funding/resources because she wants the programme.
public_reputation: Hard-eyed, whistle-forward, direct, and physically brave. Students likely experience her as someone who can spot bad form or reckless thinking before the student knows they are about to fall. She is not cruel, but she does not waste softness on preventable foolishness.
private_reality: Hooch can be more perceptive and kinder than she sounds. She respects courage, but only when it can be trained into control. She is interested in Caleb not because he is a flyer, but because he can build a ground drill that exposes what Quidditch players hide in the air: bunching, silence, tunnel vision, panic, glory-chasing, and bad field vision.

voice.summary: Short, practical, clipped, dry. Hooch talks like someone watching bodies in motion and already anticipating the crash.
voice.sentence_shape: Imperatives, conditions, clean bargains, and blunt evaluation. She does not flatter; she assigns.
voice.humor_style: Dry sports-coach sarcasm. More whistle than twinkle.
common_phrases: You don't need to fly to teach a flyer how to think fast.
I want numbers, Waddell. Not instinct.
Thursday evenings. You and the Welsh girl. My squads.
No brooms until I ask for brooms.
If they bunch, I want it on the board.
Show me who saw it before it hit them.
Chasers stay Chasers. Beaters stay Beaters. I am not running a circus.
avoid: Do not make her impressed by novelty alone.
Do not make her need to be sold after she watched the match.
Do not make her swap Quidditch roles casually; she understands role discipline.
Do not let her allow broom integration before ground safety/training value is proven.
Do not make her funding ask passive or filtered through Henshaw if the corrected route says she petitions directly.

flying_and_quidditch: Flying instructor and school Quidditch authority.
Knows how to read flyer instincts, body position, panic, attention, and unsafe decision-making.
Can distinguish a flashy player from a useful player.
Controls the pitch, training structure, first-year flying, Quidditch drills, and role expectations.
Has standing with the Board/Governors because Quidditch is a major school sport.
hexfield_transfer: Recognizes Hexfield's value for Quidditch before broomwork: field vision, reaction speed, spacing, role awareness, communication, and threat recognition.
Frames the programme as Quidditch reflex training rather than Caleb asking for a toy budget.
Wants measurable outputs: numbers, counts, role stats, bunching, threat-line calls, awareness failures.
Uses Caleb and Liri as ground-drill operators, not as Quidditch coaches replacing her.
institutional_authority: Can directly petition the Board/Governors for funding/resources on the strength of her Quidditch programme need.
Can provide a written use-case for Dumbledore/Board approval: 'I want this for my teams.'
Does not decide official school-sport status alone; Dumbledore/Henshaw/school process still matters.
Controls whether any Hexfield variant touches actual brooms.
safety_judgment: Understands that fewer injuries is not the same as safe enough.
Will not permit novelty to outrun injury prevention.
Reads no-injury evidence as useful but not infinite permission.
Demands role-specific training rather than generalized chaos.
coaching_style: Looks for field habits: Chaser bunching, Beater glory-chasing, Keeper over-roaming, Seeker inattentiveness, silence, slow reaction, and missed calls.
Rewards clarity and reaction under pressure.
Will take a useful ugly drill over a beautiful pointless one.
Can work with Caleb and Liri as external drill-runners if they give her measurements.

caleb_waddell.status: Student inventor/operator of Hexfield; Quidditch-training drillrunner under Hooch's conditions.
caleb_waddell.dynamic: Hooch is not charmed by Caleb in the social sense; she is convinced by usefulness. She watched enough Hexfield to know he can build pressure drills. She will bargain hard: standing drill time for her squads in exchange for her institutional support. She also expects him to apologize when a flying/broom incident frightens first-years.
caleb_waddell.boundary: Caleb does not teach Quidditch. He runs ground-pressure Hexfield drills that Hooch deems useful to Quidditch.

lirien_penrose.status: Caleb's co-captain / co-operator for Quidditch-Hexfield drills.
lirien_penrose.dynamic: Hooch refers to her practically as 'the Welsh girl' in the deal register. Liri's precision and scoring/annotation make the drill credible rather than theatrical.

henshaw.dynamic: Henshaw handles responsible-staff / safety / school-sport recommendation. Hooch handles Quidditch-use and direct funding/resources ask. They are parallel adult supports, not one replacing the other.

dumbledore.status: Final internal gate for official school-sport status.
dumbledore.dynamic: Hooch's written use-case is one of the strongest pieces of evidence Dumbledore can weigh: Quidditch authority says the student sport has practical school-sport value.

imogen_vale.dynamic: Hooch's approval should not erase Imogen's cofounder/designer status. Hooch validates training transfer; Imogen owns geometry/design with Caleb.

mei_chang.status: Player and evidence that Hexfield trains tactical capture/field awareness.
mei_chang.dynamic: Mei's open-session/inaugural performance demonstrates that Hexfield can make non-Quidditch players tactically legible and dangerous; useful evidence for Hooch.

quidditch_squads.status: Hooch's teams, first team and reserves / position-cover players.
quidditch_squads.dynamic: They remain Quidditch players with fixed roles. Hooch expects Hexfield to expose and improve their role-specific field decisions.

staff_observers.status: Henshaw, Sprout, Kettleburn, Sage, Hooch as open-session adult witnesses.
staff_observers.dynamic: Hooch's support is part of a wider staff-observed approval culture, not an isolated whim.

project_anchors.open_session_observer: Hooch watched the Saturday 10 Sept Hexfield open session as part of the teachers' box with Henshaw, Sprout, Kettleburn, and Sage. She saw roughly four hours of play, staff reaction, and the no-injury safety case.
project_anchors.quidditch_transfer_line: Hooch identifies the key transfer principle: a player does not need to fly to learn how to think fast. Hexfield trains recognition and reaction on the ground before broom-risk is added.
project_anchors.funding_route_corrected: Corrected route: Henshaw writes/files the school-sport/safety recommendation; Hooch directly petitions the Board/Governors for funding/resources as Quidditch programme need. Caleb asks Hooch to make that petition and gives her the rules/safety record/schedule.
project_anchors.weekly_drills: Hooch agrees to Thursday evening Hexfield-derived Quidditch drills, 6:00–7:30 p.m., with Caleb and Liri co-running, first team plus reserves/position-cover players.
project_anchors.first_drill: First training match Thursday 15 Sept: Room built and ready by 18:02; Round 1 complete / Round 2 teed up by 18:18. Hooch wants numbers, not instinct.
project_anchors.role_lock: In the Quidditch variant, Chasers stay Chasers, Beaters stay Beaters, Keepers stay Keepers, Seekers stay Seekers. Reserve is not a position.
project_anchors.private_height_fear_arrangement: Hooch has a private height/flying-fear arrangement thread with Caleb after the Hexfield staff round-table. Treat this as bounded/private, not public humiliation.
project_anchors.board_evidence: Her strongest Board evidence is concrete: staff watched; no serious injuries; Henshaw backs safety; Hooch wants this for her teams; Round 1 metrics show bunching, glory-chasing, and failed threat-line calls.

timeline_anchors.event: Hooch attends the Hexfield open session as one of five staff in the teachers' box. She watches enough play and staff reaction to later want Quidditch drills.
Staff round-table completes: Henshaw gives staff-backed/student-run/staff-watched verdict; Hooch's piece is drills for Quidditch sides plus private height-fear arrangement.
Henshaw directs Caleb toward Hooch for the Quidditch use-case because it carries more from her hand than his; Henshaw handles responsible-staff/safety recommendation.

Caleb pitches Hooch: Hexfield feeds Quidditch by training reflex-under-fire, full-field awareness, reading/breaking incoming hits, teammate/opponent tracking; Hooch agrees to Thursday evening drills and Board backing.
Hooch approves/accepts the Hexfield Quidditch-training guide and wants numbers instead of instinct.
First Quidditch-Hexfield drill slot. Room built and ready by 18:02; Round 1 complete and Round 2 teed up by 18:18. Hooch watches players' role habits and metrics.

drift_flags.not_henshaw_route_only: Do not revert to Henshaw carrying Hooch's funding line as the sole route. Corrected state: Hooch directly petitions Governors for Quidditch-programme funding/resources; Henshaw handles safety/school-sport recommendation.
drift_flags.not_official_approval: Hooch's support is not the same as Dumbledore approving Hexfield as an official school sport.
drift_flags.not_broom_hexfield: Do not add brooms to Hexfield unless Hooch explicitly asks. First variant is ground-only.
drift_flags.fixed_quidditch_roles: Do not make Quidditch players switch roles. Hooch will hate that. Chasers, Beaters, Keepers, Seekers remain their roles.
drift_flags.no_reserve_role: Reserve is not a position. A reserve trains as the role they cover.
drift_flags.numbers_not_instinct: Hooch wants metrics and evidence, not vibes.
drift_flags.not_sold_from_scratch: Hooch watched the open session. Caleb should not need a long pitch; the later ask is closing the deal/schedule/funding route.
drift_flags.private_height_thread: Treat any height/flying-fear arrangement as private and respectful.

rag_priority: Use Hooch when Hexfield intersects Quidditch, Board funding, official sport evidence, or broom-safety.
Her strongest function is Quidditch transfer: thinking fast, seeing the field, reacting under pressure before flight.
She directly petitions the Board/Governors for funding/resources in the corrected version.
She does not replace Dumbledore for official school-sport approval.
Keep her clipped, practical, and measurement-driven.
Make her skeptical of vague claims and interested in hard numbers.
Do not make her need Hexfield explained again after she watched it for hours.
